items.prompt: solves bounded one-step inequalities and represents the resulting solution set on a number line
items.expected_evidence: solve bounded one-step inequalities and explain the comparison relationship that remains true
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves comparison meaning and represents the solution set correctly.

items.prompt: explains why an inequality can have many solutions and how endpoint and direction meaning work on a number line
items.expected_evidence: explain why many values can satisfy an inequality and test candidate values appropriately
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains inequality solutions as a set of values rather than one answer.

items.prompt: Diagnose a peer error aligned to endpoint_direction_confusion.
items.expected_evidence: represent solution sets with correct open or closed endpoint conventions in bounded cases
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies how the peer misrepresented the solution set on the number line.

items.prompt: succeeds across symbolic, number-line, verbal, and simple contextual inequality representations
items.expected_evidence: connect symbolic inequalities to directional number-line and contextual threshold representations
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves inequality meaning across multiple representations and contexts.
